# 24to12Converter.com

> A free, modern 24-hour to 12-hour time converter with bidirectional conversion, programmatic time pages in 9 languages, and a growing library of time-format guides.

## Project Overview

- **URL:** https://www.24to12converter.com/
- **Stack:** HTML5, Tailwind CSS 4, vanilla JavaScript (ES6+), Lucide Icons
- **Deployment:** GitHub Pages (static HTML, no server)
- **Languages:** English (root), German (/de/), Spanish (/es/), French (/fr/), Portuguese (/pt/), Italian (/it/), Japanese (/ja/), Korean (/ko/), Traditional Chinese (/zh-tw/)
- **Git:** main branch, commit history documents iterative SEO and content improvements

## Core Tools

| Page | URL | Description |
|------|-----|-------------|
| 24h to 12h Converter (EN) | `/` | Main bidirectional time converter, English |
| Military Time Converter (EN) | `/military-time-converter/` | Specialized military time tool with printable chart |
| Time Conversion Chart (EN) | `/time-chart/` | Full 24h-to-12h reference table (00:00–23:50) |

Each language version has its own homepage, military-time-converter, time-chart, and guides.

## English Guides

These are hand-written HTML articles targeting long-tail informational queries. Each has unique meta, JSON‑LD Article + BreadcrumbList schema, direct-answer box, tables, and cross-links to tools and other guides.

1. `/guides/24-hour-clock-vs-12-hour-clock/` — 24‑Hour Clock vs 12‑Hour Clock: comparison, conversion method, examples
2. `/guides/am-and-pm-meaning/` — AM and PM Meaning: what the abbreviations stand for, when each period starts
3. `/guides/12-am-vs-12-pm/` — 12 AM vs 12 PM: midnight vs noon, the common confusion, 24‑hour equivalents
4. `/guides/how-to-read-24-hour-time/` — How to Read 24‑Hour Time: step‑by‑step rules, speaking aloud
5. `/guides/military-time-chart-printable/` — Printable Military Time Chart: full‑day reference, print button
6. `/guides/how-to-pronounce-military-time/` — How to Pronounce Military Time: zero‑hundred, oh‑six‑hundred, 0000 vs 2400
7. `/guides/why-the-us-uses-12-hour-clock/` — Why the US Uses the 12‑Hour Clock: history, British influence, country comparison table
8. `/guides/24-hour-time-around-the-world/` — 24‑Hour Time Around the World: region‑by‑region usage, travel tips
9. `/guides/what-does-0000-mean/` — What Does 00:00 Mean?: midnight explained, 00:00 vs 2400, common contexts

## Translated Guides

Non-English guides are translations of the most universally relevant English articles. Currently 2 per language:

| Language | Hub | Articles |
|----------|-----|----------|
| German | `/de/guides/` | 24‑h vs 12‑h, AM/PM Meaning |
| Spanish | `/es/guides/` | AM/PM Meaning, 12 AM vs 12 PM |
| French | `/fr/guides/` | AM/PM Meaning, 12 AM vs 12 PM |
| Portuguese | `/pt/guides/` | 24‑h vs 12‑h, 12 AM vs 12 PM |
| Italian | `/it/guides/` | 24‑h vs 12‑h, 12 AM vs 12 PM |
| Japanese | `/ja/guides/` | 24‑h vs 12‑h, 12 AM vs 12 PM |
| Korean | `/ko/guides/` | 24‑h vs 12‑h, 12 AM vs 12 PM |
| Trad. Chinese | `/zh-tw/guides/` | 24‑h vs 12‑h, 12 AM vs 12 PM |

## Programmatic Time Pages

864 static HTML pages generated at 15‑minute intervals (00:00, 00:15, 00:30, 00:45 … 23:45) in all 9 languages.

- Pattern: `/{lang?}/time/HH-MM/`
- Each page has: unique title, unique meta description, direct‑answer block, nearby‑time navigation, popular‑time links, breadcrumbs, BreadcrumbList JSON‑LD, and (since 2026‑06‑15) a reduced 3‑QA FAQPage schema.
- Generator: `generate_multilingual_pages.js` (not in public repo — reads a language homepage as template and replaces content blocks)

## SEO / Structured Data

- Every page has Open Graph, Twitter Card, and canonical URL metadata.
- Tool pages: `WebSite` + `SoftwareApplication` JSON‑LD.
- Guide articles: `Article` JSON‑LD with datePublished/dateModified.
- Time pages: `FAQPage` JSON‑LD (3 QAs), `BreadcrumbList` JSON‑LD.
- Guide hubs: `CollectionPage` JSON‑LD.
- `sitemap.xml` includes all tool pages, guide hubs, and guide articles.
- Hreflang tags on all localized pages for language alternates.

## Key Files for Contributors

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `index.html` | English homepage (also the template for English time pages) |
| `script.js` | Client‑side conversion logic |
| `src/input.css` | Tailwind CSS source |
| `dist/output.css` | Compiled Tailwind CSS |
| `generate_multilingual_pages.js` | Generates all 864 programmatic time pages |
| `generate_time_pages.js` | Older English‑only time‑page generator |
| `audit_site_refined.js` | Static‑analysis auditor (dead links, canonical, hreflang) |
| `update_sitemap.js` / `update_sitemap_multilingual.js` | Sitemap generators for time pages |
| `sitemap.xml` | Full sitemap (tools + guides + time pages) |
| `plans.md` | SEO optimization plan and progress tracker |
| `todo.md` | Development roadmap (AdSense prep, new tools, etc.) |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Project documentation for Claude Code |
| `llms.txt` | This file — site overview for LLMs |

## Writing Conventions

- Answer the search query in the first two sentences.
- Short paragraphs (3–4 sentences max), short sentences.
- No filler openings, no marketing language.
- Concrete examples for every rule; at least one table per guide.
- Internal links use natural anchor text, not raw URLs.
- All articles are dated; guides show "Updated Month DD, YYYY".

## Current Priorities (June 2026)

1. Improve US CTR through meta description rewrites and FAQPage reduction (implemented 2026‑06‑15).
2. Publish one long‑tail guide article per day to capture informational queries.
3. Monitor GSC for CTR changes over the next 28 days.
4. Add Spanish and Portuguese guide expansions when time‑page CTR gains are indexed.

## Build Commands

```bash
# Build CSS
npx tailwindcss -i ./src/input.css -o ./dist/output.css --watch

# Production build
npx tailwindcss -i ./src/input.css -o ./dist/output.css --minify

# Generate all time pages
node generate_multilingual_pages.js

# Audit site
node audit_site_refined.js
```

---

*Last updated: 2026-06-15*
